nl_query: For each question paper, show the paper title, the number of sections, the number of leaf questions, the number of attempt rules configured, the number of approved answer keys, and the number of boards using it, ordered by boards using it descending.
failed_sql: SELECT qp.title, COUNT(DISTINCT qs.id) AS num_sections, COUNT(DISTINCT q.id) AS num_leaf_questions, COUNT(DISTINCT ar.id) AS num_attempt_rules, COUNT(DISTINCT ak.id) AS num_approved_answer_keys, COUNT(DISTINCT b.id) AS num_boards FROM question_paper qp JOIN question_section qs ON qs.qp_id = qp.id JOIN question q ON q.section_id = qs.id JOIN attempt_rule ar ON ar.question_id = q.id JOIN answer_key ak ON ak.qp_id = qp.id JOIN board b ON b.qp_id = qp.id GROUP BY qp.title ORDER BY num_boards DESC;
error: Validation failed (semantic): SQL applies COUNT(qs.id) but 'qs.id' is also a GROUP BY key. COUNT over a GROUP BY key is tautological: SUM returns the value itself, COUNT(DISTINCT) always returns 1. Use a different column for the aggregation, or restructure the query.
